title: Trichosporum bracteatum
taxonomy: Trichosporum bracteatum, also known as the misopate or the yellow water lily, belongs to the family Haemodoraceae and is classified as a flowering plant. This plant is native to tropical Australia, specifically in the eastern regions, including Queensland and New South Wales. Trichosporum bracteatum is further classified as a member of the genus Trichosporum, which is a small group of plants within the Haemodoraceae family. The plant's classification places it as a monocot, indicating its unique characteristics and growth patterns. As a flowering plant, Trichosporum bracteatum is also classified as an angiosperm, further highlighting its botanical classification.
